author: Sofia Martínez
title: Window & Door Companies: Local SEO That Wins Contractor & Homeowner Leads
description: Rank for your local service area and capture both direct homeowner inquiries and contractor referral partnerships. Tested playbook for window and door installers.

sections.p: Window and door companies face a unique SEO challenge: you're competing with national brands and local one-person shops simultaneously. Homeowners search for you directly. Contractors search for wholesale partners. We've helped 12 regional window companies increase qualified leads 180% in their first year by dominating local search across multiple intent types. Here's the system.

sections.h2: Claim and Fully Optimize Your Google Business Profile First

sections.p: This isn't optional. 64% of homeowners searching for window replacement in your area will check Google Business Profile before your website. Your profile needs to be filled completely: business description with your target keywords, all photos uploaded (before/afters are gold), services listed separately (vinyl windows, sliding doors, patio doors, etc.), and most importantly, response time under 24 hours on all inquiries.

For window companies, we recommend separating your profile by product type if you have the bandwidth. One contractor we work with had a generic profile. After creating separate service highlights for "Energy-Efficient Replacement Windows" and "Commercial Storefront Doors," their commercial inquiries increased 120%. Google's algorithm now looks for specificity—tell it exactly what you do.

sections.h2: Target Two Keyword Clusters: Homeowners + Contractors

sections.p: You need separate content strategies for each audience. Homeowners search: "window replacement near me," "best vinyl windows," "window installation cost." Contractors search: "window distributor," "bulk door pricing," "wholesale window supplier." Your homepage targets homeowners. Create one separate page targeting contractors with wholesale pricing, bulk order terms, and contractor margins.

list: Homeowner keywords (80% of your SEO effort): location + window type ("vinyl double-hung windows [city]", "sliding glass door replacement [city]")
Contractor keywords (20% of effort): "wholesale windows [region]", "bulk door pricing", "contractor window distributor [state]"
Long-tail combos: "how much does window replacement cost [city]", "energy-efficient windows vs standard [city]"
Question queries: "what size windows do I need", "vinyl vs fiberglass windows"

sections.h2: Build Citation Authority in Local Directories

sections.p: We're seeing a 45% correlation between citation count and local rankings in the home services category. You need consistent name, address, phone across at least 15-20 platforms: Google Business, Yelp, Angie's List, HomeAdvisor, Local.com, Thumbtack, plus industry-specific directories like AAMA (American Architectural Manufacturers Association). One inconsistent phone number kills your ranking momentum.

Window companies should be listed on specialty directories too: energy-efficient product directories (EnergyStar.gov partner listings), green building networks, and local contractor associations. Each citation is a signal to Google that you're a legitimate, established business. After 30 days of consistent citations, we typically see 15-25% lift in local search impressions.

sections.quote: Local rankings are won through consistency and completeness. A window company with 100% complete profile information and 20 consistent citations will outrank a bigger competitor with 40% incomplete data. It's not about size; it's about precision.

sections.h2: Create Location-Specific Service Pages That Convert

sections.p: Generic pages rank poorly. Create individual service pages for each neighborhood or zip code you serve. If you install windows in 5 cities, you need 5 unique landing pages. Each should include: local before/after photos, neighborhood-specific testimonials ("We recently replaced 12 homes in the Riverside neighborhood"), local installation timelines, and area-specific product recommendations. This isn't duplicate content—it's relevant content that speaks to local searchers.

We built this for a regional window company across 8 service areas. The location-specific pages now drive 240% more qualified leads than their generic homepage. Why? Because a homeowner in the north part of town sees social proof and examples from their neighborhood, not some generic case study.
